title: Twisha Sharma death: Mother-in-law’s lawyer loses cool, beats vehicle bonnet; watch
content: Lawyer for Twisha Sharma's mother-in-law, Giribala Singh, Advocate Enosh George, had a heated exchange with journalists outside the residence. After his vehicle allegedly ran over his foot, George accused reporters of creating a dangerous situation and questioned the impact of their legal responses in the ongoing dowry death investigation.

title: Delhi HC pulls up WFI for declaring Vinesh Phogat ‘ineligible’ for domestic events
content: The Delhi high court has strongly criticized the Wrestling Federation of India for declaring Vinesh Phogat ineligible for domestic events. The court urged the Centre to form an expert committee to assess Phogat's status and ensure her participation in Asian Games trials, emphasizing the protection of athletes and the sport's larger interest.

title: Jhalmuri vendor who served PM Modi facing death threats from Pak, B'desh
content: A jhalmuri vendor from Jhargram, who served Prime Minister Modi, claims to be receiving threatening calls from Pakistan and Bangladesh.  The vendor, Vikram Sao, has informed the police, who have provided security.  Sao, the sole breadwinner, fears for his safety while cycling and has appealed to the PM for protection after his interaction went viral.

title: ‘Now that cremation is over...’: Ex-cricketer’s shocking disclosure about commentary
content: Former cricketer Laxman Sivaramakrishnan revealed he was compelled to commentate shortly after his brother's cremation, highlighting immense professional pressure. He also cited this and alleged colour discrimination as reasons for his recent retirement from the BCCI commentary panel, detailing past struggles with mental health and appearance-based bias.

title: ‘Pushed off terrace for dowry’: Deepika Nagar’s family demands fast-track justice
content: A 24-year-old woman died after falling from her in-laws' terrace in Greater Noida, with her family alleging dowry torture and demanding a fast-track trial. Police have arrested four individuals, including her husband and in-laws, while three others remain absconding. Autopsy revealed severe injuries, and viscera samples are undergoing forensic analysis.

title: Microsoft AI CEO gave these white-collar jobs 12-18 months before they are fully automated
content: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman predicts white-collar jobs like lawyers and accountants could be fully automated within 12-18 months. He emphasizes AI will transform roles, shifting humans to oversight and strategic tasks, similar to how software engineers now use AI for coding.  Doctors, for instance, may focus more on patient care and emotional support as AI handles diagnoses.

title: 'All negativity will go away, if...': Ponting responds to social media backlash
content: Punjab Kings coach Ricky Ponting dismisses social media criticism amid a six-match winless streak, asserting the team remains focused. He believes qualifying for the playoffs will silence negativity and urges players to recall their early-season fearless brand of cricket to revive their fading hopes against Lucknow Super Giants.

title: Lucknow doctor accused of raping ailing student after sending father, staff out of OT
content: A doctor at a private Lucknow hospital is accused of raping a female student after administering a sedative injection during treatment. The student alleged the doctor called her into the operation theatre alone and assaulted her after giving her an intoxicating injection. Police have registered a case and detained the doctor for questioning.

title: 'People offering condolences': Twisha's mother-in-law denies speaking to influencial people
content: Retired judge Giribala Singh denies allegations of contacting influential figures after her daughter-in-law Twisha Sharma's death. She claims calls were for condolences and accuses local police of pressure. Sharma's family alleges dowry harassment and abetment to suicide. The husband, Samarth Singh, remains absconding. The case involves FIRs under the Bharatiya Nyay Sanhita and Dowry Prohibition Act.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.

title: Sudharsan, Gill script IPL history, break multiple records in CSK demolition
content: Sai Sudharsan and Shubman Gill etched their names in IPL history with remarkable performances against Chennai Super Kings. Both batters surpassed 600 runs for the second consecutive season, a feat achieved by only a select few. Their dominant opening partnership also saw them climb various T20 and IPL record lists.

title: 'Muttatthengu' in TN: DMK takes 'bent coconut tree' jibe at VCK over CM Vijay's cabinet
content: A literary spat erupts in Tamil Nadu politics as DMK leader A Raja's 'coconut tree' metaphor targets VCK and IUML joining CM Vijay's new government. VCK and the ruling TVK hit back, accusing the DMK of arrogance and questioning their own past alliances and commitment to social justice.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: Diabetes in the scrolling era: Are screens becoming a hidden risk?
content: Prolonged screen time poses significant risks for individuals with diabetes, contributing to sedentary lifestyles, poor sleep, and increased snacking. This can lead to uncontrolled blood sugar, weight gain, and long-term complications like diabetic retinopathy. Experts advise incorporating movement breaks, limiting screen time before bed, and mindful technology use to manage these risks.

title: RBI steps in with heavy dollar sales to defend rupee: Report
content: The Reserve Bank of India aggressively intervened, selling billions to defend the rupee against sustained pressure.  This significant dollar selling, even before markets opened, helped the currency strengthen.  Elevated oil prices remain a key concern, with policymakers considering further measures, including a potential interest rate hike, to stabilize the rupee.

title: 'Content, not individuals': SC tones down remarks against academicians in NCERT row
content: The Supreme Court has assured three academics that harsh remarks against them in a previous order will be deleted. These remarks stemmed from a Class 8 NCERT chapter on judicial corruption. The court clarified its concern was the chapter's content, not the individuals, emphasizing the need for balanced academic discussions on the judiciary while respecting constitutional frameworks.

title: 'Sometimes people abuse so much': Ashwin defends Gaikwad amid criticism
content: Ravichandran Ashwin has defended Ruturaj Gaikwad against fan criticism following Chennai Super Kings' IPL 2026 playoff exit. Ashwin urged supporters to stop abusing the young captain, emphasizing that poor form is common and highlighting the increased competitiveness of the IPL. He noted Gaikwad's struggles as both batter and captain during the season.

title: ‘No sacrifice of cows, calves’: Delhi govt warns against illegal slaughter before Bakrid
content: Delhi Minister Kapil Mishra has directed strict enforcement of animal welfare laws for Bakrid.  He emphasized zero tolerance for illegal animal transport, slaughter, and cruelty.  Sacrifices are permitted only at designated sites, with a complete ban on cows and camels.  Public spaces are off-limits for animal trade and sacrifice, with swift action promised against violators.

title: ‘Ghar pe toh judge Twisha hi hogi’: Playful wedding videos surface as police hunt absconding husband
content: A wedding trailer of Twisha Sharma and Samarth Singh, once a joyous glimpse into their marriage, now haunts online amid a sensational dowry death case. Twisha's family accuses her husband and retired judge mother-in-law of harassment. The video contrasts sharply with the grim reality of police investigations and court battles, as the husband remains absconding.

title: ‘Only MS will know, but...’: CSK coach drops big hint on Dhoni’s IPL future
content: MS Dhoni's IPL future rests solely with him, according to CSK bowling coach Eric Simons. Despite a leg injury sidelining him from IPL 2026, Dhoni is reportedly striking the ball brilliantly in nets. Simons emphasised that Dhoni will only return if he feels he can contribute fully, prioritising the team's best interests over personal milestones.

title: Head to head: India and Pakistan's nuclear missile arsenal
content: In a dramatic display of military strategy, Operation Sindoor witnessed India targeting Pakistani airbases and intercepting their missiles, ultimately facilitating a quick resolution. This incident emphasizes the delicate nuclear dynamics between India and Pakistan, two nations holding nearly equivalent stockpiles. Despite their varied approaches to weapon usage, both countries utilize a nuclear triad, essential for their deterrence capabilities.

title: 'You have to save yourself': Sanjay Dutt’s daughter Trishala on her painful childhood
content: Trishala Dutt, daughter of Sanjay Dutt, reveals a difficult childhood in New York. She faced racism and bullying from a young age. The loss of her mother at eight deeply affected her. Dutt found solace in journaling and later, a pivotal conversation with her aunt empowered her. She pursued psychology and became a therapist, using her experiences to help others.

title: Umar Khalid gets 3-day interim bail for mother’s surgery
content: Umar Khalid has been granted three days of interim bail by the Delhi High Court to be with his mother for her surgery. He will be released from June 1 to June 3, with strict conditions including staying within Delhi-NCR and only visiting the hospital. The court's decision came after a trial court rejected his temporary relief plea.

title: SRH vs RCB: Bat first or lose? Pitch trend adds huge twist in today's IPL clash
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad host Royal Challengers Bengaluru in a crucial IPL 2026 playoff qualifier. The winner secures a top-two finish, with the Hyderabad pitch expected to favor batting first in this night match. SRH holds a slight head-to-head advantage, but RCB has a strong away record.

title: Bought something defective? Here is how you can take a company to consumer court
content: Indian consumers now have a streamlined path to justice for defective products and poor services. The Consumer Protection Act, 2019, empowers individuals to file complaints with consumer commissions, bypassing lengthy civil court procedures.  Cases are categorized by value, with online filing available, offering a swift resolution for grievances.

title: ‘If 60 wickets aren’t enough, scrap Ranji’: Vengsarkar slams selectors over Nabi snub
content: Cricket legend Dilip Vengsarkar is absolutely fuming! He's slammed the selectors for completely snubbing Auqib Nabi, the Ranji Trophy's top bowler, for the Afghanistan Test.  Despite Nabi's incredible 60 wickets and being named Player of the Tournament for leading J&K to their first title, they apparently passed him over due to 'pace concerns'.

title: 'We do not want him in politics': Parents of CJP founder fear arrest
content: Parents of 'Cockroach Janta Party' founder Abhijeet Dipke express deep anxiety over his viral political satire platform, fearing legal trouble and arrest.  Dipke, a Boston University student, launched the campaign a week ago, amassing millions of followers. His parents urge him to abandon politics for a safer career, citing his own expressed fears of detention upon returning to India.

title: Spying for Pakistan: Punjab man held for using highway CCTV to track Army movement
content: A Pathankot resident, Baljit Singh, has been arrested for allegedly spying for Pakistan-based handlers. He reportedly installed an internet-based CCTV camera near a bridge on NH-44 to monitor Army and paramilitary movements, transmitting live feeds to his handlers. Singh confessed to receiving directions from Dubai and being paid Rs 40,000.

title: Madhya Pradesh govt hands over CBI probe into Twisha Sharma's death
content: Madhya Pradesh government recommended a CBI probe into Twisha Sharma's death amid allegations. Her mother-in-law, former judge Giribala Singh, faces potential anticipatory bail cancellation and review of her commission presidency. Police issued a final notice to Singh, while her absconding son seeks anticipatory bail.

title: Inside 'Drishyam 3' star Mohanlal’s peaceful Kochi residence
content: Mohanlal's Kochi residence, a blend of nature and modern design, is drawing attention alongside 'Drishyam 3's box office triumph. The actor's home features extensive use of wood, a serene verandah with a sculpture, vibrant festival decorations, luxurious aquariums, and a swimming pool. His passion for antiques is evident throughout the elegantly designed interiors.

title: Oil to cross $200 per barrel? Report flags worst-case Hormuz scenario that Iran warned of
content: A prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could trigger a severe global energy crisis, potentially pushing oil prices to $200 per barrel by late 2026.  Wood Mackenzie's report outlines three scenarios, with the worst-case predicting a significant economic contraction.  This warning echoes Iran's own earlier predictions amid escalating regional tensions.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' OTT release date in India: When and where to watch Ranveer's film
content: Ranveer Singh's blockbuster 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge,' a global box office hit, is now heading to JioHotstar in India. After an exclusive premiere on June 4th at 7 PM, subscribers can stream the action-packed spy thriller from June 5, 2026. This follows its international OTT release and theatrical run. Read the full article to know more in detail.

title: 'Quran mandates qurbani': Humayun Kabir stirs row after Bengal cattle slaughter curb
content: A political storm erupted in West Bengal as AJUP chief Humayun Kabir vowed to conduct cow slaughter for Eid, defying state restrictions. He insisted the Quran mandates it, challenging the government's notice. BJP countered, emphasizing law enforcement and opposing illegal slaughter, while some Muslim leaders urged respect for cows.

title: IMD issues red alert for 10 UP districts, warns of 'very severe' heatwave
content: Uttar Pradesh faces a severe heatwave as the IMD issues a red alert for 10 districts, warning of extreme temperatures.  An orange alert covers 34 districts, with a yellow alert for 17 more.  Temperatures are expected to rise further in eastern UP.  Authorities are on high alert, with health advisories and preparedness measures in place.

title: Kohli 58 runs away from historic IPL record; set to overtake Gayle, Warner, Rahul
content: Virat Kohli is on the cusp of a historic IPL milestone, needing just 58 runs against Sunrisers Hyderabad to become the first player with four consecutive 600-plus run seasons. He is currently tied with Chris Gayle, David Warner, and KL Rahul, who each achieved the feat in three consecutive seasons.

title: 'Where is her husband?' Twisha's brother questions after Samarth missing for 10 days
content: Twisha Sharma's family questions husband Samarth Singh's anticipatory bail plea amid dowry harassment and suicide allegations. They accuse his mother, a retired judge, of intimidation and question the delay in FIR registration. Police have issued a Look Out Circular for Samarth, who claims the prosecution's case is based on 'imagination'.

title: Excluding family from your Will won't make it invalid, Supreme Court rules
content: The Supreme Court has underscored the legal rights of individuals to dictate their estate plans, stating that a will is not automatically deemed suspicious simply because it omits heirs like a spouse or children.

title: ‘Keeps everyone on their toes’: Pat Cummins on teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Pat Cummins opens up on SRH’s fearless brand of cricket, the changing landscape of T20 batting, workload management, and surviving as an all-format fast bowler. The Australia captain also reflects on leadership, adapting to modern cricket, and the “wow” moment Vaibhav Sooryavanshi produced while taking on bowlers in the IPL

title: 42 MLAs skip oath in God’s name during Kerala assembly swearing-in ceremony
content: Kerala's 16th Legislative Assembly's swearing-in ceremony showcased diversity as MLAs chose different languages and opted for solemn affirmations or oaths in God's name. Political rivals displayed camaraderie, and CM V.D. Satheesan reiterated his full name, addressing recent caste-related criticism. A symbolic gesture of reconciliation occurred between Pro-tem Speaker G. Sudhakaran and Pinarayi Vijayan.

title: Ebola outbreak: Flights diverted, borders tightened — how countries are taking precautions
content: In response to a severe Ebola outbreak, countries worldwide are tightening travel restrictions. The US has implemented mandatory screenings for incoming passengers at Washington Dulles from areas affected by the outbreak, while Uganda has halted transportation connections with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

title: IPL: Ruturaj Gaikwad penalised after CSK’s crushing defeat to Gujarat Titans
content: Chennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad has been fined INR 24 lakh for a slow over-rate, marking their second offense this IPL 2026 season. The penalty came as Gujarat Titans crushed CSK by 89 runs, eliminating them from playoff contention. GT's strong batting, led by Gill and Sudharsan, set a massive target, while CSK's chase faltered significantly.

title: Twisha Sharma death: Madhya Pradesh  government proposes to transfer case to CBI
content: The family of Twisha Sharma, the 33-year-old model turned actor accused her in-laws of pushing their daughter to death

title: 22 Ministers aged below 50: Vijay-led Cabinet signals generational shift in Tamil Nadu politics
content: Data on TVK Ministers, as on May 21, show that the Cabinet of 51-year-old Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has the youngest average age among the three governments examined, while the 2021 Cabinet of M.K. Stalin had the oldest

title: After CM Vijay’s order to revive Amma Canteens, AIADMK hopes for comeback of other legacy schemes
content: AIADMK functionaries have urged the revival of other flagship welfare schemes introduced during the Dravidian party’s previous tenures

title: NCERT book row: Supreme Court modifies earlier order concerning 3 academics
content: The Court also observed that its comments were made in context of the contents and not the individuals

title: Supreme Court grants bail to J&K man booked under UAPA after five years in custody as undertrial
content: The bail order comes a few days after Supreme Court observed in another case that the slogan ‘bail is the rule’ is not an empty one

title: PM's gifts: Muga silk stole for Italy PM, Ladakhi stole for Sweden PM, Kesar mangoes for UAE President
content: PM Modi gifts traditional Indian artifacts and delicacies to global leaders during his five-nation diplomatic tour

title: DMK faces a challenge as allies set to join TVK government
content: While the Congress abandoned the DMK soon after polls and joined Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay’s Cabinet on Thursday, the IUML and VCK are set to join the Ministry on Friday, signalling the end of their relations with the DMK

title: Ebola surveillance stepped up at Kochi airport after emergency meet
content: A Cochin International Airport Limited spokesperson said a 24-hour monitoring system has now been activated at the airport as part of efforts to detect possible symptoms early and strengthen coordinated public health response measures

title: CM Yogi asks officials to take investment to every U.P. district
content: CM Yogi Adityanath emphasised expanding the land bank, adding that the incentives provided for investment should be turned into events to honour investors and boost their morale.

title: 2025 landslide in Shimla Act of God, NHAI tells NGT, rejects compensation claim
content: NHAI submitted its reply against plea filed in January, claiming that heavy construction work being undertaken by the authority and its concessionaire, without properly strengthening a slope, had damaged agricultural land in Shimla rural tehsil, causing landslides

title: CM Vijay-led Cabinet in Tamil Nadu expands to 33; Congress joins government after 59 years
content: Mr. Vijay’s Cabinet is likely to undergo one more round of expansion, as the TVK has extended an open invitation to the VCK and IUML, both of which contested as part of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance (SPA) and have two MLAs each

title: Hyderabad Ponzi case: Enforcement Directorate arrests Nowhera Shaik
content: The officials said she was living in Gurugram under a ‘fake identity using forged documents’ and was produced before Prevention of Money Laundering Act court in Hyderabad

title: Room for reconciliation within AIADMK
content: A senior leader of the rebels, who include former Ministers C.Ve. Shanmugam, S.P. Velumani and G. Hari, says that efforts were on “from the beginning” to bring the two camps together

title: Sharmila criticises Modi over Melody toffees gifted to Italian PM Giorgia Meloni
content: The APCC president calls the gesture “an insane and insensitive display of showmanship” and says BJP leaders hand out chocolates while people struggle with rising prices

title: Shashi Tharoor slams X ban on ‘Cockroach Janata Party’
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor criticised the withholding of CJP’s X account, saying democracies need space for satire, dissent and youth frustration

title: Infants in Telangana have better survival odds, IMR at 17 against India’s 24
content: Kerala remained the best-performing large State in the country with an IMR of 8, followed by Delhi and Tamil Nadu at 11 each

title: Why your daily cup of tea is getting costlier in Kerala
content: Rising commercial cooking gas prices have raised tea shop costs. Come June 1, Milma will also hike milk prices by ₹4/litre, further compounding the problem

title: Will not only stop infiltration but also deport each and every infiltrator, says Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister said “High powered demography mission will be announced soon, it will make vulnerable locations available to BSF”

title: KTR demands white paper on procurement all Rabi crops
content: The BRS leader argues that not more than 35% of the total paddy produced as procured so far; demands ex-gratia to all farmers who died due to delay in procurement

title: Centre asks States to step up Ebola preparedness after WHO-declares global health emergency
content: The Ministry has also circulated a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) covering disease surveillance, sample collection, storage and referral mechanisms for suspected Ebola cases

title: Manithaneya Makkal Katchi resolves to contest on its own symbol in future elections in Tamil Nadu
content: The MMK was allotted two seats by the DMK – Manapparai and Nagappatinam – and it contested both on the DMK’s ‘Rising Sun’ symbol. This prevented the party from exploring any post-poll alliance, as it is bound by the DMK whip’s direction

title: Delhi HC pulls up Wrestling Federation over decision to declare Vinesh Phogat ‘ineligible’
content: The court asked the Centre to form expert committee to evaluate Ms. Phogat to ensure her participation in upcoming Asian Games selection trials.

title: Election Commission of India announces Rajya Sabha polls for 24 seats on June 18
content: ECI said the elections for the Rajya Sabha seats will be held in 10 States where the incumbents are retiring on different dates beginning June 21 to July 19

title: VCK, IUML join Vijay’s Cabinet in Tamil Nadu
content: With VCK’s Vanni Arasu and IUML’s Shahjahan on board, Vijay’s Cabinet in Tamil Nadu now has 35 Ministers

title: Visakhapatnam MP Sribharat meets TDP cadre; ward reorganisation, port dust among issues raised
content: Party workers from the North Assembly Constituency flag ward reorganisation changes, the Urban Land Ceiling issue, port dust and heavy vehicles on city roads at the meeting

title: Karnataka revises liquor prices. What does it mean and why your beer might get cheaper
content: As the state rolls out a new excise duty structure for alcohol, we break down what it means, and speak to bar owners and brands about how it will affect prices in Bengaluru

title: Karnataka's new excise regime: What are the revised alcohol prices?
content: With the new prices kicking in, lower-end liquor in Karnataka has become dearer while certain premium labels have become cheaper.

title: Stalin urges DMK cadre to avoid harsh criticism over VCK, IUML joining TVK government
content: Mr. Stalin’s remarks came against the backdrop of some DMK leaders criticising the entry of the VCK and the IUML, both of which contested as part of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance, into the TVK-led Cabinet

title: Driver charred to death in bus-truck collision on Agra-Lucknow Expressway
content: Twenty passengers were injured in the accident, of whom seven seriously injured persons were admitted to Firozabad hospital, while others were referred to Saifai, says Firozabad SP

title: Delhi riots 2020 case: Umar Khalid gets three-day interim bail
content: Umar Khalid had sought interim bail to attend a post-death ritual (Chehlum) of his uncle and take care of his mother, who has to undergo a surgery.

title: Calcutta HC orders setting up SIT of CBI to probe hushing up charges in R G Kar rape-murder case
content: The Bench said that, considering the social ramifications of the gruesome incident, a three-member SIT would look into charges of evidence destruction and allegations made by the medic's parents that there were attempts to hush up and downplay the incident.

title: Corporations begin setting up disaster response teams in Bengaluru as monsoon approaches
content: The move comes in the wake of recent rains in the city that caused substantial destruction, including the uprooting of 515 trees and the snapping of over 1,500 branches

title: Jagan flays govt. over fuel prices, Amaravati debt
content: YSRCP chief also criticises CM's call to have more children and accuses TDP-led govt. of encouraging ‘gun culture'

title: JSP’s T.C. Varun condemns political analyst Nageswar’s remarks against ruling NDA alliance
content: Addressing the media in Anantapur, the Jana Sena leader demands that the former MLC withdraw his comments and warns of a backlash from alliance cadres

title: Fire sweeps through many shops in Uttar Pradesh’s Hardoi market; causes two blasts
content: The fire personnel first removed four gas cylinders kept inside the shops to prevent further explosions

title: Twisha Sharma death: Final notice served to ex-judge Giribala Singh for recording statement
content: Twisha Sharma’s family lawyer Ankur Pandey said that a petition had been filed before the principal Bench of the Madhya Pradesh High Court in Jabalpur challenging the anticipatory bail granted to Giribala Singh.

title: Historic shift: Vijay’s Cabinet marks Tamil Nadu’s best Scheduled Caste representation till date
content: This is the first time that seven people from the SC community have been included simultaneously in the Tamil Nadu Cabinet

title: Heavy rain alert in Kerala: Orange alert in Alappuzha, yellow in 6 districts
content: IMD has predicted widespread light to moderate rainfall with isolated instances of heavy rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds in various parts of Kerala till May 27

title: Two Indians die while descending from Mount Everest summit, says official
content: The climbers, identified as Arun Kumar Tiwari and Sandeep Are, were exhausted while they were coming down from the peak and could not be saved despite the best efforts of their guides

title: TVK-led Tamil Nadu government: full list of Ministers
content: The specific portfolios for the nine Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) ministers were previously announced on Saturday (May 16, 2026), with Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay retaining key departments

title: Hyderabad traffic | Private vehicles account for 60-70% trips
content: Remedies for traffic issues in Hyderabad, a massive drop in MMTS ridership and other aspects of public transport services in the city were discussed at a town hall

title: Veteran Congress leader Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan elected Kerala Speaker
content: In the elections held to Speaker’s post, Thiruvanchoor won 101 votes, while A.C. Moideen of Opposition LDF and B.B. Gopakumar of BJP-led NDA won 35 and 3 votes respectively. Speaker formally announces Pinarayi Vijayan as Opposition Leader

title: Jharkhand police seize narcotics worth more than ₹50 lakh in two separate operations; five held
content: In Chatra district, police seized two four-wheelers and recovered 324 kg of opium husks, and in Hazaribag district, 54 grams of brown sugar was seized and five people were arrested

title: Bihar districts cut school hours as temperatures cross 40°C
content: The order further stated that classes for students from Classes 6 to 8 would be permitted only till 10.30 a.m.

title: Intermediate advanced supplementary exams begin across Andhra Pradesh; 93.4% turnout on day one
content: BIEAP Secretary P. Ranjit Basha inspects a Vijayawada centre and orders drinking water, ORS packets and medicines at all centres as the State swelters

title: Singing of 'Vande Mataram' compulsory in all schools, madrasas in Bengal: Dilip Ghosh
content: The order by the Directorate of Madrasah Education came nearly a week after the State's BJP Government made singing the national song mandatory in all schools

title: Red alert issued as heatwave sweeps Uttar Pradesh
content: The alert has been issued for Banda, Chitrakoot, Kaushambi, Prayagraj, Fatehpur, Pratapgarh, Mirzapur, Varanasi, Bhadohi and Jaunpur districts, where severe heatwave conditions are likely during the day

title: PM, Education Minister must be held accountable for 'cover up': Congress on NTA claims on NEET exam
content: The Congress also said that the National Testing Agency (NTA) has become a "National Trauma Agency" and accused the government of colluding with the paper leak mafia to suppress the truth of irregularities and fraud.

title: Agitation for separate Dharwad corporation receives more support, citizens install new board
content: The indefinite agitation demanding bifurcation of the Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation enters the seventh day

title: Andhra photographer Rukhiya Mohammed on her award-winning photographs of Eastern Ghats
content: Rukhiya Mohammed shares her thoughts on winning the Nature inFocus Award 2025 for  documenting the changing landscapes of the Eastern Ghats

title: Sports startup SWOT Sportech uses analytics and biomechanics to train young athletes
content: SWOT Sportech aims to bring elite-level sports science and performance analytics to grassroots athletes across India

title: Cops Oppose Giribala Singh's Bail, Cite Tampering, Non-Cooperation In Probe
content: The state has claimed that although CCTV footage from Giribala Singh's house had already been seized by police on May 13 as part of the investigation, the respondent had allegedly saved footage with herself.

title: On CCTV Time Lag In Twisha Sharma Case, Technician Said This
content: Twisha Sharma, a 33-year-old model-turned-actor, was found hanging at her marital home in Bhopal's Katara Hills area on May 12.

title: Why Does Every Human Voice Sound Different? Scientists Explain
content: Over time, humans lost the vocal membranes and air sacs found in monkeys and apes and developed a more stable voice box.

title: Edible Oil On Boil, Monthly Budget In Soup: Food Inflation Hits Households
content: Iran War Impact: Tensions in the Middle East and rising freight costs are once again creating pressure on global vegetable oil markets.

title: 2 Indian Climbers Die Descending Mount Everest After Summit
content: The deaths come amid successful Everest summits by several Indians, including 21-year-old Saanika Shah and the Border Security Force (BSF) all-women mountaineering team.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Twisha Sharma And India's Deadly Culture Of 'Adjusting A Little More'
content: A daughter's desperate cry for help is responded to with "adjust", "compromise", or "save the marriage". Her parents even promise to give more dowry, but refuse to bring her back. Girls are repeatedly sent back into abusive homes till one fine day, t

title: Delhi Woman Gets News Of Sister's Death 7 Minutes After "Bacha Lo, Didi" SOS
content: Veena's brother alleged that her in-laws were not satisfied with the dowry and wanted a Royal Enfield Bullet.

title: Saanika Shah, 21, Who Did 'Bharatnatyam' At Base Camp, Climbs Everest
content: Saanika Shah is an avid Bharatnatyam dancer, and her successful Mount Everest ascent is a major milestone in her dream to complete the Seven Summits challenge.

title: Inside Israel's High-Tech 'Kill-Or-Capture' Mission After October 7 Attack
content: The report says Israel is targeting people at every level, from senior Hamas commanders to individuals accused of carrying out smaller roles during the attack.

title: Terrorists' Lunch Under Tree, Attack At 2:23 PM, Celebratory Shots: How Pahalgam Horror Unfolded
content: 25 tourists and a local civilian were killed when three Pakistani terrorists stormed out of pine forests and fired on crowds in the Baisaran Valley of Jammu and Kashmir's Pahalgam on April 22.

title: In Twisha Sharma Death Case, Madhya Pradesh Government Recommends CBI Probe
content: Twisha Sharma's wedding video: Many friends and relatives speak about the couple, how they met, what kinds of people they are, apart from some jokes and friendly swipes

title: From Deve Gowda To M Kharge: Voting For 26 Rajya Sabha Seats On June 18
content: Election Commission announces Rajya Sabha polls for 26 seats across eight states with polling on June 18

title: Delhi Issues Bakrid Advisory, Bans Cow, Camel Sacrifice In Public Places
content: The advisory, issued by Delhi Development minister Kapil Mishra, also states that animal sacrifice at public places such as roads, streets and open areas will not be permitted under any circumstances.

title: "Worked Really Hard": 2 Indian Mountaineers Die While Descending Everest
content: It appears that Are summited on Wednesday and Tiwari on Thursday around 5.30 pm, said Rishi Bhandari, secretary general of the Expedition Operators Association of Nepal.

title: Did Chinese General Disguise As Waitress To Spy On Elon Musk? What Report Said
content: Zeng shared an image of two servers from the CCP's state banquet honouring President Trump, claiming photos of the same women "in uniform" were being widely circulated on Chinese social media.

title: Twisha Sharma's Family Flags Mother-In-Law's "Influential Calls" Amid Probe
content: A family statement issued by Twisha Sharma's father detailed her mother-in-law Giribala Singh's calls to several influential individuals, including IAS and IPS officers, judges, doctors, and advocates

title: Opinion: Opinion: TVK Dismantles Dravidian Stereotypes Without Disturbing Tamil Political Positions
content: A young Brahmin man, with or without any political background, winning a seat in Tamil Nadu and subsequently becoming a minister, does not fit the established template of Dravidian politics.

title: Chase, Clash, Crash: Thar Fight In Andhra Pradesh's Vijayawada Brings Traffic To Halt
content: The two men reportedly chased each other in Thar before crashing on the road and engaging in a violent confrontation in Andhra Pradesh's Vijayawada.
